<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Warranty Reserves </b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company generally provides a <em data-td-fact-id="25969932" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25969933" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year warranty against defects in materials and workmanship and will either repair the goods or provide replacement products at <em data-td-fact-id="25969934" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> charge to the customer for defective products. Reserve requirements are recorded in the period of sale and are based on an assessment of the products sold with warranty and historical warranty costs incurred. Historically, the warranty expenses have <em data-td-fact-id="25969935" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> been material to the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s consolidated financial statements.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Revenue Recognition </b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s revenue consists primarily of product sales of assembled and tested integrated circuits and dies in wafer form to the consumer, computing and storage, industrial, automotive and communications markets. The remaining revenue has <em data-td-fact-id="25969936" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> been significant historically and primarily includes royalties from licensing arrangements and revenue from wafer testing services for <em data-td-fact-id="25969937" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> parties.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">T<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">he Company recognizes revenue based on the following <em data-td-fact-id="25969938" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> criteria: (<em data-td-fact-id="25969939" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) persuasive evidence of an arrangement exists; (<em data-td-fact-id="25969940" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) delivery has occurred or services have been rendered; (<em data-td-fact-id="25969941" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>) the fee is fixed or determinable; and (<em data-td-fact-id="25969942" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>) collectability is reasonably assured. Determination of criteria (<em data-td-fact-id="25969943" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>) and (<em data-td-fact-id="25969944" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>) is based on management&rsquo;s judgment regarding the fixed nature of the fees charged for products delivered and the collectability of those fees. The application of these criteria has resulted in the Company generally recognizing revenue upon shipment or delivery (when title and risk of loss have transferred to customers), including to most of the distributors, original equipment manufacturers and electronic manufacturing service providers.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> each of the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25969945" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25969946" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25969947" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">88%</em> of the Company&rsquo;s sales, respectively, were made through distribution arrangements with <em data-td-fact-id="25969948" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> parties. The Company generally recognizes revenue upon shipment or delivery of products to the distributors based on the following considerations:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969949" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The price is<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> fixed or determinable at the date of sale. The Company does <em data-td-fact-id="25969950" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> offer special payment terms&nbsp;</font>(the<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Company&rsquo;s normal payment terms are <em data-td-fact-id="25969951" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">30</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25969952" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">45</em> days for its distributors)&nbsp;or price adjustments to distributors </font>when<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;the Company recognizes revenue upon shipment or delivery.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969953" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The distributors are<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> obligated to pay the Company and this obligation is <em data-td-fact-id="25969954" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> contingent on the resale of the&nbsp;</font>Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s products.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969955" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The distributors<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo; obligation is unchanged in the event of theft or physical destruction or damage to the products.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969956" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The distributors has<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> stand-alone economic substance apart from the Company&rsquo;s relationship.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969957" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company does<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> <em data-td-fact-id="25969958" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have any obligations for future performance to directly bring about the resale of its products&nbsp;</font>by the distributors.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.7%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25969959" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.1%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Th<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">e amount of future returns can be reasonably estimated. </font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Certain of the C<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ompany&rsquo;s large distributors have contracts that included limited stock rotation rights that permit the return of a small percentage of the previous <em data-td-fact-id="25969960" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">six</em> months&rsquo; purchases. The Company maintains a sales reserve for stock rotation rights, which is based on historical experience of actual stock rotation returns on a per-distributor basis and information related to products in the distribution channel. This reserve is recorded at the time of sale. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="25969961" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the reserve for stock rotation rights was <em data-td-fact-id="25969962" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.6</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25969963" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.9</em> million, respectively.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">If the Company enters<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> into arrangements with distributors that have price adjustment or other rights that are <em data-td-fact-id="25969964" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> fixed or determinable, the Company recognizes revenue under such arrangements only after the distributors have sold the products to end customers, at which time the price is <em data-td-fact-id="25969965" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> longer subject to adjustment and is fixed.&nbsp;Three of the Company&rsquo;s U.S.-based distributors have such price adjustment rights and accordingly, the Company defers revenue recognition on these shipments until the products are sold to the end customers by the distributors. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="25969966" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the deferred&nbsp;revenue balance before the price adjustments from these distributors was <em data-td-fact-id="25969967" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.9</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25969968" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.7</em> million, and the deferred costs were <em data-td-fact-id="25969969" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.2</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25969970" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.3</em> million, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018, </em>the Company adopted Accounting Standar<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ds Update (&ldquo;ASU&rdquo;) <em data-td-fact-id="25969971" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25969972" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25969973" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em><i> </i><i>Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606</i><i>)</i>. The primary&nbsp;effects of the new standard for the Company related to the timing of revenue recognition with the <em data-td-fact-id="25969975" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> U.S.-based distributors with price adjustment rights.&nbsp;See &ldquo;Recent Accounting Pronouncements <em data-td-fact-id="25969976" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">Not</em> Yet Adopted as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017&rdquo; </em>below for further discussion.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Stock-Based Compensation </b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company measures the cost of employee services received in exchange for an award of equity instruments based on the grant-date fair value of the award.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i>&nbsp;The fair value of restricted stock units (&ldquo;RSUs&rdquo;) with service conditions or performance conditions is based on the grant date share price. The fair value of RSUs with only market conditions, as well as RSUs containing both market and performance conditions, is estimated using a Monte Carlo simulation model.&nbsp; The fair value of stock options, shares issued under the employee stock purchase plan and RSUs with a purchase price feature is estimated using the Black-Scholes model.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">53</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Compensation expense related to awards with service conditions is recorded on a straight-line basis over the requisite service period. Compensation expense related to awa<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">rds subject to market or performance conditions is recognized over the requisite service period for each separately vesting tranche. For awards with only market conditions, compensation expense is <em data-td-fact-id="25969977" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> reversed if the market conditions are <em data-td-fact-id="25969978" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> satisfied. For awards with only performance conditions, as well as awards containing both market and performance conditions, the Company recognizes compensation expense when it becomes probable that the performance criteria will be achieved. Management performs the probability assessment on a quarterly basis by reviewing external factors, such as macroeconomic conditions and the analog industry forecasts, and internal factors, such as the Company&rsquo;s business and operational&nbsp;objectives and revenue forecasts. Changes in the probability assessment of achievement of the performance conditions are accounted for in the period of change by recording a cumulative catch-up adjustment as if the new estimate had been applied since the service inception date. Any previously recognized compensation expense is reversed if the performance conditions are <em data-td-fact-id="25969979" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> expected to be satisfied.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Prior to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>the<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> Company recognized stock-based compensation expense less an estimate for forfeitures. Upon the adoption of ASU <em data-td-fact-id="25969980" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25969981" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25969982" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em><i> </i><i>Compensation&mdash;Stock Compensation (Topic 718): Improvements to Employee Share-Based Payment Accounting</i><i>,</i> on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>the Company elected to account for forfeitures when they occur. See &ldquo;Recently Adopted Accounting Pronouncement&rdquo; below for further discussion.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Research and Development </b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Costs incurred in research and devel<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">opment are expensed as incurred.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Accounting for Income Taxes</b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company recognizes federal, state and foreign current tax liabilities or assets based on its estimate of taxes payable or refundable in the current fiscal year by tax jurisdiction. The Company also recognizes federal, state and foreign deferred tax assets or liabilities for its estimate of future tax effects attributable to temporary differences and carryforwards. The Company records a valuation allowance to reduce any deferred tax assets by the amount of any tax benefits that, based on available evidence and judgment, are <em data-td-fact-id="25969984" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> expected to be realized.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s calculation of current and deferred tax assets and liabilities is based on certain estimates and judgments and involves dealing with uncertainties in the application of complex tax laws. The Company&rsquo;s estimates of current and deferred tax assets and liabilities <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>change based, in part, on added certainty or finality or uncertainty to an anticipated outcome, changes in accounting or tax laws in the U.S. or foreign jurisdictions where the Company operates, or changes in other facts or circumstances. In addition, the Company recognizes liabilities for potential U.S. and foreign income tax for uncertain income tax positions taken on its tax returns if it has less than a <em data-td-fact-id="25969985" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> likelihood of being sustained. If the Company determines that payment of these amounts is unnecessary or if the recorded tax liability is less than its current assessment, the Company <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be required to recognize an income tax benefit or additional income tax expense in its financial statements in the period such determination is made. The Company has calculated its uncertain tax positions which were attributable to certain estimates and judgments primarily related to transfer pricing, cost sharing and its international tax structure exposure.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 22, 2017, </em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">the tax legislation commonly known as the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (the <em data-td-fact-id="25969986" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2017</em> Tax Act&rdquo;) was enacted, which significantly changed&nbsp;U.S. corporate income tax law. </font>The <em data-td-fact-id="25969987" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act made the following material changes<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">:<i><b> </b></i>(<em data-td-fact-id="25969988" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) reduction of the corporate income tax rate effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018; (</em><em data-td-fact-id="25969989" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) replacement of the worldwide tax system with a territorial tax regime, with a <em data-td-fact-id="25969990" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-time mandatory tax on previously deferred foreign earnings; (<em data-td-fact-id="25969991" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>) amendment on the deductibility of executive performance-based compensation, and (<em data-td-fact-id="25969992" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>) creation of new taxes on certain foreign-sourced earnings.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Income tax effects resulting from changes in tax laws are accounted for by the Company in the period in which the law is enacted. <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">See Note <em data-td-fact-id="25969993" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em> for further&nbsp;discussion.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">54</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Litigation and </b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>Contingencies</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is a party to actions and proceedings in the ordinary course of business, including potential litigation regarding its shareholders and its intellectual property, challenges to the enforceability or validity of its intellectual property, claims that the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s products infringe on the intellectual property rights of others, and employment matters. The pending proceedings involve complex questions of fact and law and will require the expenditure of significant funds and the diversion of other resources to prosecute and defend. In addition, from time to time, the Company becomes aware that it is subject to other contingent liabilities.&nbsp;When this occurs, the Company will evaluate the appropriate accounting for the potential contingent liabilities to determine whether a contingent liability should be recorded. In making this determination, management <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may, </em>depending on the nature of the matter, consult with internal and external legal counsel and technical experts. Based on the facts and circumstances in each matter, the Company uses its judgment to determine whether it is probable that a contingent loss has occurred and whether the amount of such loss can be estimated. If the Company determines a loss is probable and estimable, the Company records a contingent loss. In determining the amount of a contingent loss, the Company takes into account advice received from experts for each specific matter regarding the status of legal proceedings, settlement negotiations, prior case history and other factors. Should the judgments and estimates made by management need to be adjusted as additional information becomes available, the Company <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>need to record additional contingent losses. Alternatively, if the judgments and estimates made by management are adjusted, for example, if a particular contingent loss does <em data-td-fact-id="25969996" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> occur, the contingent loss recorded would be reversed.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Litigation expense (benefit), net in the Consolidated Statements of Operations includes primarily patent infringement litigation and other business matters. The Company records litigation costs in the period in which they are incurred. Proceeds resulting from settlement of litigation or favorable judgments are recorded as a reduction against litigation expense.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Net Income p</b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>er Share</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Basic net income per share is computed by dividing net income by the weighted-average number of common shares outstanding for the period. Diluted net income per share reflects the potential dilution that would occur if outstanding securities or other contracts to issue common stock were exercise<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">d or converted into common shares, and calculated using the treasury stock method.&nbsp;Contingently issuable shares, including equity awards with performance conditions or market conditions, are considered outstanding common shares and included in the basic net income per share as of the date that all necessary conditions to earn the awards have been satisfied. Prior to the end of the contingency period, the number of contingently issuable shares included in the diluted net income per share is based on the number of shares, if any, that would be issuable under the terms of the arrangement at the end of the reporting period.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s outstanding RSUs contain forfeitable rights to receive cash dividend equivalents, which are accumulated and paid to the employees when the underlying RSUs vest. Dividend equivalents accumulated on the underlying RSUs are forfeited if the employees do <em data-td-fact-id="25969997" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> fulfill their service requirement and the awards do <em data-td-fact-id="25969998" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> vest. Accordingly, these awards are <em data-td-fact-id="25969999" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> treated as participating securities in the net income per share calculation.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Comprehensive Income</b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Comprehensive income represents the change in the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s net assets during the period from non-owner sources. Accumulated other comprehensive income (loss) presented in the Consolidated Balance Sheets primarily consists of unrealized gains or losses related to available-for-sale investments and foreign currency translation adjustments.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Recently Adopted Accounting Pronouncement</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Stock-Based Compensation:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;<em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 2016, </em>the Financial Accounting Standards Board (&quot;FASB&quot;) issued ASU&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970000" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970001" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970002" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em>&nbsp;<i>Compensation&mdash;Stock Compensation (Topic 718): Improvements to Employee Share-Based Payment Accounting,</i>&nbsp;which changed how entities account for certain aspects of share-based payment awards, including the accounting for&nbsp;excess tax benefits and tax deficiencies, forfeitures, statutory tax withholding requirements, as well as classification of excess tax benefits in the statements of cash flows. The Company adopted the standard on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017 </em>and the primary impact of the adoption was as follows:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.5%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Company elected to account for forfeitures of equity awards when they occur. The change was applied on a </font>modified<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;retrospective basis and the Company recorded a cumulative-effect adjustment of <em data-td-fact-id="25970004" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.1</em> million&nbsp;to retained </font>earnings<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;on&nbsp;<em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017,&nbsp;</em>with a corresponding offset to additional paid-in capital.&nbsp;</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:36pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.5%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Excess tax benefits are recognized in the income tax provision in the Consolidated Statements of Operations prospectively, rather than in additional paid-in capital in the Consolidated Balance Sheets. The Company applied the modified retrospective method and there was <em data-td-fact-id="25970005" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> net impact&nbsp;on&nbsp;retained earnings on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>as the increase in deferred tax assets related to previously unrecognized excess tax benefits was fully offset by a valuation allowance.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:1.8%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:96.5%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is presenting excess tax benefits as an operating activity in the Consolidated Statements of Cash<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Flows on </font>a prospective basis.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">55</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><i>Recent Accounting Pronouncements Not&nbsp;Yet Adopted as of December 31, 2017</i></b></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Revenue Recognition:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May 2014, </em>the <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">FASB&nbsp;issued ASU <em data-td-fact-id="25970007" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970008" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970009" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em>&nbsp;<i>Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606),&nbsp;</i>which outlines a single comprehensive model for entities to use in accounting for revenue arising from contracts with customers. The standard&rsquo;s core principle is that an entity will recognize revenue when it transfers promised goods or services to customers in an amount that reflects the consideration to which the entity expects to be entitled in exchange for those goods or services. The Company adopted the standard on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018 </em>using the modified retrospective method and prior-period results will <em data-td-fact-id="25970011" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be restated. In the <em data-td-fact-id="25970012" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970013" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> the Company will record approximately <em data-td-fact-id="25970014" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.8</em> million, before tax, to retained earnings&nbsp;related to the cumulative effect of adopting Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970015" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606,</em> primarily due to the change in revenue recognition for its U.S.-based distributors as discussed below</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970016" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, the Company&rsquo;s product sales consist of a single performance obligation that is satisfied at a point in time. The Company recognizes product revenue from distributors and other customers when the products are shipped or delivered to the customers (based on the terms of the firm purchase orders and sales agreements), primarily because (a) the Company has transferred physical possession of the products, (b) the Company has a present right to payment, (c) the customers have legal title to the products, and (d) the customers bear significant risks and rewards of ownership of the products. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">T<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">he primary&nbsp;change for the Company under Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970017" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606</em> relates to the timing of revenue recognition with <em data-td-fact-id="25970018" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> U.S.-based distributors. Sales to these distributors are transacted under the terms of agreements providing price adjustment and other rights. Prior to the adoption of Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970019" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606,</em> revenue and costs related to these sales were deferred until the Company received&nbsp;notification from the distributors that products have been sold to the end customers and the amount of price adjustments was fixed and finalized. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the deferred&nbsp;revenue balance before the price adjustments was <em data-td-fact-id="25970020" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.9</em> million and the related deferred costs were <em data-td-fact-id="25970021" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.2</em> million. Upon adoption of Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970022" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606,</em> the transaction price takes into consideration the effect of variable consideration such as price adjustments, which are estimated and recorded at the time the promised goods are transferred to the customers. Accordingly, effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018, </em>the Company recognizes revenue at the time of shipment or delivery to the distributors, adjusted for an estimate of the price adjustments based on management&rsquo;s review of historical data and other&nbsp;information available at the time. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Revenue from other U.S. and&nbsp;non-U.S. distributors, which make up the majority of the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s total sales to distributors, was already recognized at the time of shipment or delivery to the distributors prior to the adoption of Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970023" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606</em> because these arrangements do <em data-td-fact-id="25970024" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> contain price adjustment rights. Accordingly, revenue recognition on arrangements with these&nbsp;distributors remains substantially unchanged upon adoption of Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970025" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Other</u><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><u>:</u></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the FASB issued ASU<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970028" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970029" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970030" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">02,</em>&nbsp;<i>Leases (Topic 842),</i>&nbsp;which requires entities to recognize a right-of-use asset and a lease liability on the balance sheets for substantially all leases with a lease term greater than <em data-td-fact-id="25970032" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em> months, including leases currently accounted for as operating leases. </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">In addition, the standard applies to leases embedded in service arrangements. The standard requires modified retrospective adoption and will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2018, </em>with early adoption permitted. The Company is evaluating the impact of the adoption on its consolidated financial position, results of operations, cash flows and disclosures.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2016, </em>the FASB issued ASU <em data-td-fact-id="25970033" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970034" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970035" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13,</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;<i>Financial Instruments &ndash; Credit Losses (Topic 326): Measurement of Credit Losses on Financial Instruments,&nbsp;</i>which introduces a model based on expected losses to estimate credit losses for most financial assets and certain other instruments. In addition, for available-for-sale debt securities with unrealized losses, the losses will be recognized as allowances rather than reductions in the amortized cost of the securities. The standard will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2019, </em>with early adoption permitted for annual reporting periods beginning after <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2018. </em>Entities will apply the standard by recording a cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings. The Company is evaluating the impact of the adoption on its consolidated financial position, results of operations, cash flows and disclosures.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 2017, </em>the FASB issued ASU <em data-td-fact-id="25970037" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970038" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970039" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">04,</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;<i>Intangibles &ndash; Goodwill and Other (Topic 350): Simplifying the Accounting for Goodwill Impairment,</i>&nbsp;which simplifies the accounting for goodwill impairment. The guidance removes step <em data-td-fact-id="25970041" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> of the goodwill impairment test, which requires a hypothetical purchase price allocation. A goodwill impairment will now be the amount by which a reporting unit&rsquo;s carrying value exceeds its fair value, <em data-td-fact-id="25970042" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> to exceed the carrying amount of goodwill. Entities will continue to have the option to perform a qualitative assessment to determine if a quantitative impairment test is necessary. The standard will be applied prospectively, and is effective for annual reporting periods beginning after <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2019. </em>Early adoption is permitted. The Company is evaluating the impact of the adoption on its annual goodwill impairment test.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">56</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">

</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6337" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132397463" id="thunderdome-statement-770167">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970043" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.</em> ACQUISITION</b></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 22, 2014 (</em>the &ldquo;Acquisition Date&rdquo;), the Company acquired <em data-td-fact-id="25970044" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">100%</em> of the outstanding capital stock of Sensima Technology SA (&ldquo;Sensima&rdquo;) for total purchase consideration o<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">f <em data-td-fact-id="25970045" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$14.2</em> million, which consisted of a cash payment of <em data-td-fact-id="25970046" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$11.7</em> million and the fair value of contingent consideration of <em data-td-fact-id="25970047" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.5</em> million. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The contingent consideration arrangement required the Company to pay up to an additional <em data-td-fact-id="25970048" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.9</em> million to former Sensima shareholders if Sensima achieved a new product introduction as well as certain product revenue and direct margin targets in <em data-td-fact-id="25970049" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> The fair value of the contingent consideration at the Acquisition Date was <em data-td-fact-id="25970050" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.5</em> million, which was estimated based on a probability-weighted analysis of possible future<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;revenue outcomes. The fair value of the contingent consideration was initially recorded in other long-term liabilities in the Consolidated Balance Sheets and was remeasured at the end of each reporting period, with any changes in fair value recorded in operating expense in the Consolidated Statements of&nbsp;Operations. As part of the quarterly assessment in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970051" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970052" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> management reviewed the sales forecast for the products and determined that the projected product revenue in <em data-td-fact-id="25970053" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> would likely <em data-td-fact-id="25970054" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> meet the minimum target required to earn the contingent consideration, primarily because the product adoption process by customers would take longer than the Company had originally anticipated. Accordingly, the fair value of the contingent consideration was deemed to be <em data-td-fact-id="25970055" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0</em> as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015. </em>The Company released the liability of <em data-td-fact-id="25970056" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.5</em> million and recorded the credit in selling, general and administrative expenses for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015. </em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">at the conclusion of the performance period, management affirmed that <em data-td-fact-id="25970057" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> contingent consideration was earned as the actual product revenue in <em data-td-fact-id="25970058" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> did <em data-td-fact-id="25970059" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> meet the minimum target.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6338" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132405464" id="thunderdome-statement-770168">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970060" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp;</b><b>CASH, CASH EQUIVALENTS AND INVESTMENTS</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The following is a summary of the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s cash, cash equivalents and short-term and long-term investments (in thousands):&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Level <em data-td-fact-id="25970076" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&mdash;includes instruments for which the valuations are based upon quoted market prices in active markets involving </font>similar<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;assets or inputs&nbsp;other than quoted prices that are observable for the assets.&nbsp;The market inputs used to value these </font>instruments<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;generally consist of market yields, recently executed transactions, broker/dealer quotes or alternative pricing sources </font>with<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;reasonable levels&nbsp;of price transparency. Pricing sources <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>include industry standard data providers, security master files&nbsp;</font>from<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;large financial institutions, and other <em data-td-fact-id="25970077" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em>-party sources used to determine a daily market value.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970087" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>. GOODWILL AND ACQUISITION-REL</b><b>A</b><b>TED INTANGIBLE ASSETS, NET</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">There have be<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">en <em data-td-fact-id="25970088" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> changes in the goodwill balance for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="25970089" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> The Company did <em data-td-fact-id="25970090" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> identify any goodwill impairment for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970091" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970092" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015.</em></font></p>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6342" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132455175" id="thunderdome-statement-770190">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970098" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp;</b><b>STOCK-BASED COMPENSATION</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><i><i><em data-td-fact-id="25970099" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em></i> Equity Incentive Plan (the <i><em data-td-fact-id="25970100" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2004</em></i> Plan&rdquo;)</i></b></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Board of Directors adopted the <em data-td-fact-id="25970101" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em> Plan in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 2004, </em>and the stockholders approved it in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2004. </em>The <em data-td-fact-id="25970102" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em> Plan provided for annual increases in the number of shares available for issuance equal to the least of <em data-td-fact-id="25970103" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5%</em> of the outstanding shares of common stock on the <em data-td-fact-id="25970104" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> day of the year, <em data-td-fact-id="25970105" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.4</em> million shares, or a number of shares determined by the Board of Directors. The <em data-td-fact-id="25970106" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em> Plan expired on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 12, 2014, </em>and equity awards can <em data-td-fact-id="25970107" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> longer be granted under the <em data-td-fact-id="25970108" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em> Plan. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 12, 2014, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970109" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.9</em> million shares that were available for issuance expired under the <em data-td-fact-id="25970110" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em> Plan.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b><i><em data-td-fact-id="25970111" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em></i> Equity Incentive Plan (the <i><em data-td-fact-id="25970112" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2014</em></i> Plan&rdquo;)</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Board of Directors adopted the <em data-td-fact-id="25970113" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Plan in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2013, </em>and the stockholders<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;approved it in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2013. </em>In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> October 2014, </em>the Board of Directors approved certain amendments to the <em data-td-fact-id="25970114" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Plan. The <em data-td-fact-id="25970115" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Plan, as amended, became effective on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 13, 2014 </em>and provides for the issuance of up to <em data-td-fact-id="25970116" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5.5</em> million shares. The <em data-td-fact-id="25970117" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Plan will expire on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 13, 2024. </em>As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017,&nbsp;</em><em data-td-fact-id="25970118" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3.2</em> million shares remained available for future issuance under the <em data-td-fact-id="25970119" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Plan.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Stock-Based Compensation Expense</b></i></p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">61</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In the <em data-td-fact-id="25970123" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970124" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s then Chief Financial Officer retired. As the service or performance conditions for her outstanding restricted stock units (&ldquo;RSUs&rdquo;) had <em data-td-fact-id="25970125" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> been satisfied at the time of her departure, the Company reversed previously accrued stock-based compensation expenses of <em data-td-fact-id="25970126" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.9</em> million associated with the unvested RSUs&nbsp;and this credit was reflected in selling, general and administrative expenses for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>RSUs</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Company&rsquo;s RSUs include time-based RSUs, RSUs with only performance conditions (&ldquo;PSUs&rdquo;), RSUs with both market and performance conditions (&ldquo;MPSUs&rdquo;), and RSUs with only market conditions (&ldquo;MSUs&rdquo;). Vesting of all awards requires continued service for the Company. In addition, vesting of awards with performance conditions or market conditions is subject to the achievement of pre-determined performance goals. A summary of RSU activity&nbsp;is presented in the table below (in thousands, except per-share amounts):&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
