<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">_________________</font></p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="width:18pt;vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">(<em data-td-fact-id="25970127" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>)</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-size:10pt;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;">Amount reflects the number of PSUs and MPSUs that <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>ultimately be earned based on management&rsquo;s probability assessment of the achievement of performance conditions at each reporting period. In addition, MPSUs are subject to the achievement of market conditions.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The intrinsic<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;value related to RSUs released was <em data-td-fact-id="25970128" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$74.0</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970129" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$62.9</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970130" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$49.2</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970131" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970132" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> respectively. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the total intrinsic value of all outstanding&nbsp;RSUs was <em data-td-fact-id="25970133" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$430.6</em> million, based on the closing stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970134" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$112.36.</em> As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>unamortized compensation expense related to&nbsp;all outstanding RSUs was <em data-td-fact-id="25970135" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$75.9</em> million with a weighted-average remaining recognition period of approximately <em data-td-fact-id="25970136" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em>&nbsp;years.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Time-Based RSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the years ended Decemb<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">er <em data-td-fact-id="25970137" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">31,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970138" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970139" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970140" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors (the &quot;Compensation Committee&quot;) granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970141" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">81,000,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970142" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">133,000</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970143" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">271,000</em>&nbsp;RSUs, respectively, with time-based vesting conditions&nbsp;to employees and non-employee directors. The RSUs generally vest over <em data-td-fact-id="25970144" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970145" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> years for employees and <em data-td-fact-id="25970146" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> year for directors, subject to continued service&nbsp;with the Company.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970147" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2017, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970148" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200,000</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, which represent a target number of shares to be awarded based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970149" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970150" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970151" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970152" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970153" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2017</em> Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer can earn is <em data-td-fact-id="25970154" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970155" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs.&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970156" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970157" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs will vest in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970158" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970159" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> if the pre-determined performance goals are met during the&nbsp;performance period and approved by the Compensation Committee. The remaining&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970160" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs&nbsp;will vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970161" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on a quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Assuming the achievement of the highest level of performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970162" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs is <em data-td-fact-id="25970163" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$36.3</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2017, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970164" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">48,000</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of&nbsp;shares to be awarded based on the Company&rsquo;s <em data-td-fact-id="25970165" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970166" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970167" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970168" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970169" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970170" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is either <em data-td-fact-id="25970171" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200%</em> or <em data-td-fact-id="25970172" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970173" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee. <em data-td-fact-id="25970174" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970175" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs will vest in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970176" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970177" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> if the pre-determined performance goals are met during the performance period and approved by the Compensation Committee. The remaining&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970178" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;will vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970179" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on an annual or quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Assuming the achievement of the highest level of performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970180" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970181" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$7.1</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">62</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The <em data-td-fact-id="25970182" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs and the <em data-td-fact-id="25970183" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company <em data-td-fact-id="25970184" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$30</em> per share upon vesting of the shares.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Shares that do <em data-td-fact-id="25970185" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> vest will <em data-td-fact-id="25970186" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970187" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Executive PSUs and the <em data-td-fact-id="25970188" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Non-Executive PSUs using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970189" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$89.37,</em> expected term of <em data-td-fact-id="25970190" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.6</em> years, expected volatility of <em data-td-fact-id="25970191" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">28.6%</em> and risk-free interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="25970192" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.3%.</em>&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970193" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the Compensation Committee granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970194" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">285,000</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares to be awarded based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970195" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970196" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970197" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970198" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970199" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2016</em> Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was <em data-td-fact-id="25970200" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970201" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs.&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970202" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970203" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs would vest in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970204" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970205" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> if the pre-determined performance goals were met during the&nbsp;performance period and approved by the Compensation Committee. The remaining&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970206" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs&nbsp;would vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970207" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on a quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 2016, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970208" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12,000</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970209" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs to the Company&rsquo;s new Chief Financial Officer. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2018, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970210" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Ex<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ecutive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970211" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">651,000</em> shares were earned by the executive officers. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970212" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970213" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$26.1</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970214" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">64,000</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of&nbsp;shares to be awarded based on the Company&rsquo;s <em data-td-fact-id="25970215" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970216" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970217" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970218" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970219" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970220" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either <em data-td-fact-id="25970221" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200%</em> or <em data-td-fact-id="25970222" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970223" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee. <em data-td-fact-id="25970224" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970225" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs would vest in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970226" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970227" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> if the pre-determined performance goals were met during the performance period and approved by the Compensation Committee. The remaining&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970228" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs&nbsp;would vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970229" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on an annual or quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2018, </em>the Compensation Committee approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970230" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Exe<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">cutive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970231" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">128,000</em> shares were earned by the employees. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970232" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970233" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.1</em>&nbsp;million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The <em data-td-fact-id="25970234" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs and the <em data-td-fact-id="25970235" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs contain a purchase price feature, which requires the employees to pay the Company <em data-td-fact-id="25970236" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$20</em> per share upon vesting of the shares.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Shares that do <em data-td-fact-id="25970237" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> vest will <em data-td-fact-id="25970238" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be subject to the purchase price payment. The Company determined the grant date fair value of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970239" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs and the <em data-td-fact-id="25970240" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Non-Executive PSUs granted in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016 </em>using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970241" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$58.98,</em> expected term of <em data-td-fact-id="25970242" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.6</em> years, expected volatility of <em data-td-fact-id="25970243" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">31.1%</em> and risk-free interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="25970244" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.9%.</em>&nbsp;For the&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970245" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Executive PSUs&nbsp;granted in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 2016, </em>the Company used the following assumptions: stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970246" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$70.98,</em> expected term of <em data-td-fact-id="25970247" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.3</em> years, expected volatility of <em data-td-fact-id="25970248" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">29.6%</em> and risk-free interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="25970249" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.7%.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970250" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2015, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970251" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">172,000</em> PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares to&nbsp;be awarded based on the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970252" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970253" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970254" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970255" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970256" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2015</em> Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was <em data-td-fact-id="25970257" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970258" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Executive PSUs.&nbsp;In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2017, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970259" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970260" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">432,000</em> shares were earned by the executive officers. <em data-td-fact-id="25970261" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970262" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970263" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970264" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em> The remaining <em data-td-fact-id="25970265" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Executive PSUs vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970266" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on a quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970267" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970268" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$21.0</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">63</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2015, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970269" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">58,000</em> PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares to be awarded based on the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s <em data-td-fact-id="25970270" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970271" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970272" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970273" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970274" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970275" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either <em data-td-fact-id="25970276" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200%</em> or <em data-td-fact-id="25970277" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970278" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee.&nbsp;In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2017, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970279" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970280" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118,000</em> shares were earned by the non-executive employees. <em data-td-fact-id="25970281" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970282" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970283" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970284" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em> The remaining <em data-td-fact-id="25970285" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970286" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on an annual or quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970287" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970288" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.7</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970289" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> M</u><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><u>P</u><u>SUs:</u></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015, </em>the Compensation Committee<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970290" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">86,000</em>&nbsp;MPSUs to the executive officers and <em data-td-fact-id="25970291" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">41,000</em> MPSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represent a target number of shares to be awarded upon achievement of both market conditions and performance conditions (<em data-td-fact-id="25970292" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2015</em> MPSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee can earn is <em data-td-fact-id="25970293" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">500%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970294" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs. The <em data-td-fact-id="25970295" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs consist of <em data-td-fact-id="25970296" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> separate tranches with various performance periods ending on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019. </em>The <em data-td-fact-id="25970297" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> tranche contains market conditions only, which require the achievement of <em data-td-fact-id="25970298" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> stock price targets ranging from <em data-td-fact-id="25970299" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$71.36</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970300" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$95.57</em> with a performance period from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2016 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019. </em>As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> September 30, 2017, </em>all <em data-td-fact-id="25970301" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> price targets for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970302" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> tranche have been achieved and approved by the Compensation Committee.</font></p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:22.5pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">The second, <em data-td-fact-id="25970303" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970304" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranches contain both market conditions and performance conditions. Each tranche requires the achievement of <em data-td-fact-id="25970305" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> stock price targets measured against a base price equal to the greater of: (<em data-td-fact-id="25970306" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) the average closing stock price during the <em data-td-fact-id="25970307" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">20</em> consecutive trading days immediately bef<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ore the start of the performance period for that tranche, or (<em data-td-fact-id="25970308" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) the closing stock price immediately before the start of the performance period for that tranche. The stock price targets for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970309" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">second</em> tranche range from <em data-td-fact-id="25970310" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$89.56</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970311" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$106.81</em> with a performance period from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019.&nbsp;</em>As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>all <em data-td-fact-id="25970312" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> price targets for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970313" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">second</em> tranche have been achieved and approved by the Compensation Committee. The stock price targets for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970314" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> tranche range from <em data-td-fact-id="25970315" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$120.80</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970316" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$135.48</em> with a performance period from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019. </em>The stock price targets for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970317" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranche will be determined on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2018 </em>with a performance period from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2019 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019.</em></font></p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In addition, each of the second, <em data-td-fact-id="25970318" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970319" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranches requires the achievement of <em data-td-fact-id="25970320" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> of following <em data-td-fact-id="25970321" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">six</em> operating metrics:</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970322" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Successful implementation of full digital solut<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ions for certain power products.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970323" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Successful implementation, and adoption by a key customer, of an integrated<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, software-based field-oriented control&nbsp;</font>with<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;sensors to motor drivers.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970324" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Successful implementation of certain advanced power analog processes.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970325" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Successful design wins and achievement of a specific level of revenue with a global networking customer.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970326" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Achievement of a specific level of revenue with a global electronics manufacturer.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;text-indent:0;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="vertical-align:middle;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:2.2%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970327" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6.</em></p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;width:95.6%;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Achievement of a specific level of market share with certain core power products.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970328" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">none</em> of the operating metrics have<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;been achieved.</font></p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Subject to the employees<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo; continued employment with the Company, the <em data-td-fact-id="25970329" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs will fully vest on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2020 </em>if the pre-determined individual market and performance goals in each tranche are met during the performance periods and approved by the Compensation Committee. In addition, the <em data-td-fact-id="25970330" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs contain sales restrictions on the vested shares by employees for up to <em data-td-fact-id="25970331" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years.</font></p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company determined the grant date fair value of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970332" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs using a Monte Carlo simulation model with the following weighted-average assumptions: stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970333" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$61.35,</em> expected volatility of <em data-td-fact-id="25970334" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">33.2%,</em> risk-free interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="25970335" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.3%,</em> and an illiquidity discount of <em data-td-fact-id="25970336" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7.8%</em> to account for the post-vesting sales restrictions.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;Assuming the achievement of all of the required market and performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970337" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> MPSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970338" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$24.6</em> million (<em data-td-fact-id="25970339" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.3</em> million for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970340" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> tranche, <em data-td-fact-id="25970341" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.5</em> million for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970342" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">second</em> tranche, <em data-td-fact-id="25970343" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.2</em> million for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970344" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> tranche, and <em data-td-fact-id="25970345" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$6.6</em> million for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970346" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranche).</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="background-color:#FFFFFF;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the <em data-td-fact-id="25970347" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> tranche, stock-based compensation expense is<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;being recognized over the requisite service period. For the second, <em data-td-fact-id="25970348" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970349" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranches, stock-based compensation expense for each tranche is recognized depending upon the number of the&nbsp;operating metrics management deems probable of being achieved during the performance periods in each reporting period. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>based on management&rsquo;s quarterly assessment, <em data-td-fact-id="25970350" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970351" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">six</em> operating metrics were considered probable of being achieved during the performance periods. Accordingly, stock-based compensation expense is being recognized for the second, <em data-td-fact-id="25970352" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970353" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> tranches over the requisite service period.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">64</div>

</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970354" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2014, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970355" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">252,000</em> PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares to be awarded based on the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970356" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970357" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970358" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970359" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970360" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2014</em> Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was <em data-td-fact-id="25970361" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970362" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Executive PSUs.&nbsp;In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970363" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970364" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">694,000</em> shares were earned by the executive officers. <em data-td-fact-id="25970365" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970366" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970367" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970368" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> The remaining <em data-td-fact-id="25970369" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Executive PSUs vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970370" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on a quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970371" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970372" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$20.7</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2014, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970373" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">61,000</em> PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares to&nbsp;be awarded based on the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s <em data-td-fact-id="25970374" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> revenue goals for certain regions or product line divisions, or based on the Company&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970375" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year (<em data-td-fact-id="25970376" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970377" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em>) revenue growth rate compared against the analog industry&rsquo;s average <em data-td-fact-id="25970378" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>-year revenue growth rate as published by the Semiconductor Industry Association (<em data-td-fact-id="25970379" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either <em data-td-fact-id="25970380" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200%</em> or <em data-td-fact-id="25970381" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970382" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee.&nbsp;In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970383" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970384" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">103,000</em> shares were earned by the non-executive employees. <em data-td-fact-id="25970385" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970386" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970387" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">second</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970388" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> The remaining <em data-td-fact-id="25970389" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970390" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on an annual or quarterly basis. Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970391" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970392" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.7</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In connection with the acquisition of Sensima in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 2014, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970393" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.0</em> million of P<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">SUs (or <em data-td-fact-id="25970394" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">47,000</em> shares) to key Sensima employees who became employees of the Company, with the right to earn up to <em data-td-fact-id="25970395" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> equal tranches totaling <em data-td-fact-id="25970396" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.0</em> million based on the achievement of certain cumulative Sensima product revenue targets during the performance period from the acquisition date to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 2019. </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970397" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the awards subject to each revenue goal will vest immediately when the revenue goal is met during the performance period and approved by the Compensation Committee. The remaining&nbsp;shares will vest over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970398" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years. The vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company.&nbsp;These equity awards are considered arrangements for post-acquisition services and the compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970399" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> tranches is recognized over the requisite service period if it is probable that the performance goals will be met.&nbsp; As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>stock-based compensation expense of <em data-td-fact-id="25970400" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.0</em> million for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970401" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> tranche was being recognized over the requisite service period. Stock-based compensation expense for the other tranches was <em data-td-fact-id="25970402" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> being recognized as their achievement was deemed <em data-td-fact-id="25970403" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> probable as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970404" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> PSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2013, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970405" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">220,000</em> PSUs to the executive officers, which represented a target number of shares to be awarded upon achievement of certain pre-determined revenue targets in <em data-td-fact-id="25970406" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="25970407" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2013</em> Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an executive officer could earn was <em data-td-fact-id="25970408" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970409" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Executive PSUs. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2015, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970410" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970411" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">622,000</em> shares were earned by the executive officers. <em data-td-fact-id="25970412" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970413" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970414" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970415" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> and the remaining shares vested over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970416" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on a quarterly basis. Vesting was subject to the employees<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970417" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares, was <em data-td-fact-id="25970418" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$15.0</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2013, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970419" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">91,000</em> PSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares&nbsp;to be awarded upon achievement of certain pre-determined revenue targets for the Company as a whole, certain regions or product-line divisions in <em data-td-fact-id="25970420" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> (<em data-td-fact-id="25970421" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2013</em> Non-Executive PSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was either <em data-td-fact-id="25970422" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200%</em> or <em data-td-fact-id="25970423" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">300%</em> of the target number of <em data-td-fact-id="25970424" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Non-Executive PSUs, depending on the job classification of the employee. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2015, </em>the Compensation Committee&nbsp;approved the revenue achievement for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970425" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Non-Executive PSUs and a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970426" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">154,000</em> shares were earned by the non-executive employees. <em data-td-fact-id="25970427" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970428" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Non-Executive PSUs vested in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970429" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970430" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> and the remaining shares vested over the following <em data-td-fact-id="25970431" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> years on an annual or quarterly basis. Vesting was subject to the employees<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. Based on the actual achievement of the performance goals, the total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970432" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Non-Executive PSUs, excluding cancelled shares,&nbsp;was <em data-td-fact-id="25970433" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.0</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">65</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u><em data-td-fact-id="25970434" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> MSUs:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 2013, </em>the Compensation Committee<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;granted <em data-td-fact-id="25970435" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">276,000</em> MSUs to the executive officers and <em data-td-fact-id="25970436" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">84,000</em> MSUs to certain non-executive employees, which represented a target number of shares that would be awarded upon achievement of <em data-td-fact-id="25970437" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> stock price targets ranging from <em data-td-fact-id="25970438" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$40.00</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970439" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$56.00</em> during a <em data-td-fact-id="25970440" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em>-year performance period from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2014 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2018 (</em><em data-td-fact-id="25970441" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2013</em> MSUs&rdquo;). The maximum number of shares that an employee could earn was <em data-td-fact-id="25970442" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">500%</em> of the target number of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970443" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> MSUs. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015, </em>all <em data-td-fact-id="25970444" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> stock price targets have been achieved and approved by the Compensation Committee, and the employees earned a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970445" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.8</em> million shares. The <em data-td-fact-id="25970446" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> MSUs will vest quarterly from <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2019 </em>to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2023. </em>Vesting is subject to the employees&rsquo; continued employment with the Company. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company determined the grant date fair value of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970447" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> MSUs using a Monte Carlo simulation model with the following assumptions: stock price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970448" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$31.73,</em> expected volatility of <em data-td-fact-id="25970449" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">38.7%</em> and ris<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">k-free interest rate of <em data-td-fact-id="25970450" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.6%.</em>&nbsp; The total stock-based compensation cost for the <em data-td-fact-id="25970451" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> MSUs, excluding cancelled shares, is <em data-td-fact-id="25970452" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$38.2</em> million.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Stock Options</b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>&nbsp; </b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970453" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No</em> stock options were granted for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970454" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970455" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015.</em> A summary of stock option activity<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;is presented in the table below:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Total intrinsic value of options exercised was <em data-td-fact-id="25970456" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.7</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970457" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.7</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970458" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$18.6</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970459" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970460" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> respectively. Proceeds from stock option exercises were <em data-td-fact-id="25970461" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.1</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970462" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.3</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970463" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$7.7</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970464" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970465" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> respectively. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>there was <em data-td-fact-id="25970466" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> unamortized compensation expense.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b><i><em data-td-fact-id="25970467" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2004</em></i> </b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>Employee Stock Purchase Plan</b></i><i><b> (&ldquo;ESPP&rdquo;)</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under the ESPP, eligible employees <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>purchase common stock through payroll deductions. Participants <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970468" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> purchase more than <em data-td-fact-id="25970469" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2,000</em> shares in a <em data-td-fact-id="25970470" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">six</em>-month offering period or stock having a value greater than <em data-td-fact-id="25970471" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$25,000</em> in any calendar year as measured at the beginning of the offering period in accordance with the Internal Revenue Code and applicable Treasury Regulations.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;The ESPP provides for an annual increase by an amount equal to the least of <em data-td-fact-id="25970472" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1.0</em> million shares, <em data-td-fact-id="25970473" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2%</em> of the outstanding shares of common stock on the <em data-td-fact-id="25970474" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> day of the year, or a number of shares as determined by the Board of Directors.&nbsp;&nbsp;As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970475" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4.6</em> million shares were available for future issuance. The ESPP will expire in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2024.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970476" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970477" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970478" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">40,000,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970479" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">53,000</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970480" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">56,000</em> shares, respectively, were issued under the ESPP. The intrinsic value of the shares issued&nbsp;was <em data-td-fact-id="25970481" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.0</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970482" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.0</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970483" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.6</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970484" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970485" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> respectively. The unamortized expense as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>was <em data-td-fact-id="25970486" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$92,000,</em> which will be recognized through the <em data-td-fact-id="25970487" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970488" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018.</em> The Black-Scholes model was used to value the employee stock purchase rights with the following weighted-average assumptions:&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

			<td id=".trail.4" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">%</td>

			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Cash proceeds from the shares issued under the ESPP <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">were <em data-td-fact-id="25970489" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.7</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970490" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.5</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970491" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.2</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970492" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970493" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> respectively.</font></p>
