<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6343" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132463268" id="thunderdome-statement-770195">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970494" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>. STOCK REPURCHASE PROGRAM</b><b>S</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 2013, </em>the Board of Directors approved a stock repurchase program (the <em data-td-fact-id="25970495" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2013</em> Program&rdquo;) that authorized the Company to repurchase up to <em data-td-fact-id="25970496" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$100</em> million in the aggregate of its common stock through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 30, 2015. </em>In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2015, </em>the Board of Directors approved an extension of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970497" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Program through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015. </em>The <em data-td-fact-id="25970498" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Program expired on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015 </em>with a remaining unused balance of <em data-td-fact-id="25970499" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.9</em> million. Shares were retired upon repurchase under the <em data-td-fact-id="25970500" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Program.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016, </em>the Board of Directors approved a stock repurchase program (the <em data-td-fact-id="25970501" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">&ldquo;2016</em> Program&rdquo;) that authorized the Company to repurchase up to <em data-td-fact-id="25970502" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$50</em> million in the aggregate of its common stock through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016. </em>In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 2016, </em>the Board of Directors approved an extension of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970503" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Program through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em>The <em data-td-fact-id="25970504" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> Program expired on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>with a remaining unused balance of <em data-td-fact-id="25970505" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$50</em> million.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2015, </em>the Company repurchased a total of <em data-td-fact-id="25970506" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">0.6</em> million shares for <em data-td-fact-id="25970507" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$32.3</em> million, at an average price of <em data-td-fact-id="25970508" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$50.05</em> per share<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> under the <em data-td-fact-id="25970509" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> Program.&nbsp; <em data-td-fact-id="25970510" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No</em> shares were repurchased for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="25970511" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6344" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132471244" id="thunderdome-statement-770196">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970512" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">9</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b>. </b><b> </b><b>DIVIDENDS AND DIVIDEND EQUIVALENTS</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Cash </b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>Dividend Program</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2014, </em>the Board of Directors approved a dividend program<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;pursuant to which the Company intends to pay quarterly cash dividends on its common stock. Based on the Company&rsquo;s historical practice, stockholders of record as of the last business day of the quarter are entitled to receive the quarterly cash dividends when and if declared by the Board of Directors, which are payable to the stockholders in the following month.&nbsp;The Board of Directors declared the following cash dividends (in thousands, except per-share amounts):&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of D<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ecember <em data-td-fact-id="25970513" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">31,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970514" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970515" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> accrued dividends totaled <em data-td-fact-id="25970516" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.3</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970517" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.2</em> million, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:9pt;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The declaration of any future cash dividends is at the discretion of the Board of Directors and will depend on, among other things, the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s financial condition, results of operations, capital requirements, business conditions, statutory requirements of Delaware law, compliance with the terms of future indebtedness and credit facilities and other factors that the Board of Directors <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>deem relevant, as well as a determination that cash dividends are in the best interests of the stockholders. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company anticipates<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> that cash used for future dividend payments will come from its current domestic cash and cash generated from ongoing U.S. operations. In addition, the Company currently plans to repatriate cash from its Bermuda subsidiary to fund future dividends and continue to indefinitely reinvest its earnings from other foreign subsidiaries. See Note <em data-td-fact-id="25970520" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em> for further discussion.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Cash Dividend Equivalent Rights</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:9pt;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s stock plans, outstanding RSUs contain rights to receive cash dividend equivalents, which entitle employees who hold RSUs to the same dividend value per share as holders of common stock. The dividend equivalents are accumulated and paid to the employees when the underlying RSUs vest. Dividend equivalents accumulated on the underlying RSUs are forfeited if the employees do <em data-td-fact-id="25970521" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> fulfill their service requirement and the awards do <em data-td-fact-id="25970522" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> vest.&nbsp;As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="25970523" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> accrued dividend equivalents totaled <em data-td-fact-id="25970524" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.8</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970525" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.1</em> million, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">67</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6346" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132486648" id="thunderdome-statement-770200">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970527" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970528" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em></b><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp; NET INCOME PER SHARE</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The following table sets forth the computation of basic and diluted net income per share (in thousands, except per share amounts):</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The effective tax rate differs from the applicable U.S. statutory federal income tax rate as follows:</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</font></p>

			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em data-td-fact-id="25969604" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(27,354</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.3" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; padding-bottom: 1px;">)</td>
		</tr>
		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Net deferred tax assets</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em data-td-fact-id="25969605" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">15,917</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em data-td-fact-id="25969606" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">633</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.3" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970532" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> U.S. Tax Reform</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Under <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">Accounting Standards Codification (&ldquo;ASC&rdquo;) <em data-td-fact-id="25970533" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740,</em> <i>Income Taxes</i>, the effects of a new legislation are recognized upon enactment. Accordingly, the Company was required to recognize the tax effects of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970534" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act beginning in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970535" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970536" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em> </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 22, 2017, </em>the Securities and Exchange Commission issued Staff Accounting Bulletin <em data-td-fact-id="25970537" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970538" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em> (&quot;SAB <em data-td-fact-id="25970539" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118&quot;</em>), which addresses the application of ASC <em data-td-fact-id="25970540" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em> in situations when a registrant does <em data-td-fact-id="25970541" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have the necessary information available, prepared, or analyzed in reasonable detail to complete the accounting for certain income tax effects of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970542" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act. In accordance with SAB <em data-td-fact-id="25970543" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118,</em> <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">for matters that have <em data-td-fact-id="25970544" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> been completed, the Company would recognize provisional amounts to the extent that they are reasonably estimable. If a reasonable estimate cannot be determined, the Company would <em data-td-fact-id="25970545" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be required to report provisional amounts and would continue to apply ASC <em data-td-fact-id="25970546" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em> based on the provisions of the tax laws that were in effect immediately before the enactment of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970547" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">has <em data-td-fact-id="25970548" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> completed its accounting for the tax effects of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970549" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act and recorded certain provisional amounts, as discussed below, based on reasonable estimates for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em>The provisional amounts are subject to revisions, possibly materially, as the Company performs further&nbsp;analysis of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970550" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act, collects and prepares necessary financial data, continues to assess its tax positions, and interprets any additional guidance issued by the U.S. Treasury Department, Internal Revenue Service (&ldquo;IRS&rdquo;), FASB, and other standard-setting and regulatory bodies. </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">Any subsequent adjustment to these amounts will be recorded to the income tax provision in the period when the analysis is complete. The Company expects to complete the analysis within the <em data-td-fact-id="25970551" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-year measurement period ending <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 21, 2018, </em>pursuant to SAB <em data-td-fact-id="25970552" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Corporate Tax Rate</u><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><u> and Re</u><u>me</u><u>asurement of Deferred Taxes</u><u>:</u></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The <em data-td-fact-id="25970553" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act reduces the corporate tax rate from <em data-td-fact-id="25970554" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35%</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970555" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21%,</em> effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018. </em>Because ASC <em data-td-fact-id="25970556" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em> requires the effect of a change in tax laws to be recognized as of the date of enactment, th<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">e Company remeasured its deferred tax balance as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 22, 2017 </em>and recorded a provisional amount of <em data-td-fact-id="25970557" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9.8</em> million to the income tax provision as a result of the remeasurement for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">69</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Deemed Repatriation</u><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><u> Transition</u><u> Tax:</u></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">T<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">he <em data-td-fact-id="25970558" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act mandates a <em data-td-fact-id="25970559" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-time deemed repatriation transition tax of post-<em data-td-fact-id="25970560" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1986</em> undistributed foreign earnings and profits (&ldquo;E&amp;P&rdquo;) on which U.S. income taxes were previously deferred. The rate applied varies depending on whether the E&amp;P is held in liquid or non-liquid assets. A proportional deduction on the deemed repatriation results in a transition tax of <em data-td-fact-id="25970561" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">15.5%</em> for cash and liquid assets and <em data-td-fact-id="25970562" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8%</em> for non-liquid assets. The transition tax is assessed regardless of whether the Company repatriates the earnings. The transition tax is determined on the greater of E&amp;P as of <em data-td-fact-id="25970563" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> measurement dates (<em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 2, 2017 </em>or <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017). </em>The amount of cash and liquid assets is determined based on the greater of the amounts calculated using <em data-td-fact-id="25970564" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em> alternative measurement periods. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company recorded a provisional amount of <em data-td-fact-id="25970565" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$41.9</em> million related to the transition tax expense. After the utilization of R&amp;D tax credits of <em data-td-fact-id="25970566" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$18.0</em> million, the transition tax payable is <em data-td-fact-id="25970567" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$23.9</em> million. As permitted by t<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">he <em data-td-fact-id="25970568" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act, the Company has elected to pay the transition tax in installments over <em data-td-fact-id="25970569" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">eight</em> years. As a result, <em data-td-fact-id="25970570" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.9</em> million was recorded in current accrued liabilities and <em data-td-fact-id="25970571" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$22.0</em> million was recorded in long-term income tax liabilities as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Undistributed Earnings of Subsidiaries:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company previously considered the earnings in its non-U.S. subsidiaries to be indefinitely reinvested and, accordingly, recorded <em data-td-fact-id="25970572" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> deferred income ta<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">xes. Prior to the transition tax, the Company had an excess of the amount for financial reporting over the tax basis in its foreign subsidiaries including undistributed foreign earnings of <em data-td-fact-id="25970573" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$390.2</em> million. While the transition tax resulted in the reduction of the excess of the amount for financial reporting over the tax basis in its foreign subsidiaries and subjected a provisional amount of&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="25970574" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$119.7</em>&nbsp;million of undistributed foreign earnings to tax, an actual repatriation from its non-U.S. subsidiaries could be subject to additional foreign withholding taxes and U.S. state taxes.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company has analyzed its<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> global working capital and cash requirements, and has determined that it plans to repatriate cash from its Bermuda subsidiary on an ongoing basis to fund its future U.S. based expenditures and dividends. For the other foreign subsidiaries, the Company expects to indefinitely reinvest undistributed earnings to fund foreign operations and their research and development. For those undistributed foreign earnings from which the Company was <em data-td-fact-id="25970575" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> able to make a reasonable estimate of the tax effects of such repatriation, the Company has <em data-td-fact-id="25970576" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> recorded any deferred taxes or accrued for any withholding taxes or state taxes as of December 31, 2017. The Company will record the tax effects of any change in its prior assertion with respect to those undistributed foreign earnings in the period that it is <em data-td-fact-id="25970577" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> able to make a reasonable estimate, <em data-td-fact-id="25970578" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> later than the end of the measurement period under SAB <em data-td-fact-id="25970579" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em></font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income:</u></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The <em data-td-fact-id="25970580" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act subjects a U.S. parent shareholder to <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">taxation of its global intangible low-taxed income (&ldquo;GILTI&rdquo;), effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">January 1, 2018. </em>The GILTI inclusions will impact companies that have foreign earnings generated without a large aggregate foreign fixed asset base and whose earnings are being taxed at a low tax rate. GILTI is calculated based on foreign income in excess of a deemed return on tangible assets of foreign corporations. The income inclusion under GILTI is eligible for a deduction that is intended to lower the effective tax rate to <em data-td-fact-id="25970581" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10.5%</em> for taxable years <em data-td-fact-id="25970582" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970583" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2025,</em> and rise to <em data-td-fact-id="25970584" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13.125%</em> for taxable years after <em data-td-fact-id="25970585" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2025.</em> </font>The enactment of the GILTI tax will result in addition<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">al Subpart F income recognition for the Company in <em data-td-fact-id="25970586" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> and onwards.&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><u>Executive Compensation</u><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><u> Deductions</u><u>:</u></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The <em data-td-fact-id="25970587" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act retains the <em data-td-fact-id="25970588" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1</em> million limitation on deductible compensation to covered employees, which include the Chief Executive Officer and <em data-td-fact-id="25970589" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">four</em> other highest paid officers, under IRC Section <em data-td-fact-id="25970590" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">162</em>(m). However, it eliminates the exception for performance-based cash or stock compensation and expands the definition of covered employees to include the Chief Financial O<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">fficer. Accordingly, beginning <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018, </em>the deductible compensation to covered employees will generally be subject to the <em data-td-fact-id="25970591" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1</em> million limitation.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Release of Valuation Allowance</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Management periodically eva<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">luates the realizability of the Company&rsquo;s deferred tax assets based on all available evidence. The realizability of the Company&rsquo;s net deferred tax assets is dependent on its ability to generate sufficient future taxable income during periods prior to the expiration of tax attributes to fully utilize these assets. The Company maintained a full valuation allowance on its U.S. deferred tax assets as of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970592" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970593" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em>&nbsp;&nbsp;</font>In the <em data-td-fact-id="25970594" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970595" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> the Company assessed the realizability of the deferred tax assets and concluded that <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">it was more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="25970596" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that its federal deferred tax assets would be realizable</font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, due principally to the enactment of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970597" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> Tax Act.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">70</div>

<hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000">
<div id="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;"><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">In accordance with ASC <em data-td-fact-id="25970598" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740,</em> management considered all available evidence, both positive and negative, to determine whether, based on the weight of that evidence, a valuation allowance for deferred tax asset was needed. The Company&rsquo;s conclusion was primarily driven by the following positive evidence:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="width:18pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td style="width:18pt;vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">The Company <font style="font-size:10pt;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;">forecasts taxable income in the U.S.&nbsp;in future periods.&nbsp; The&nbsp;</font>enactment of GILTI will result in additional Subpart F income each year.</p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="width:18pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td style="width:18pt;vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-size:10pt;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;">Executive performance-based equity awards are now subject to the Section <em data-td-fact-id="25970599" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">162</em>(m) deduction limitation.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">

		<tr>
			<td style="width:18pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td style="width:18pt;vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#9679;</p>
			</td>
			<td style="vertical-align:top;">
			<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">The Company has a history of utilizing all federal tax attributes<font style="font-size:10pt;font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;"> before expiration.</font></p>
			</td>
		</tr>

</table>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">As a result, t<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">he Company released <em data-td-fact-id="25970600" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$21.6</em> million of valuation allowance on federal deferred tax assets, which was recorded as a benefit in the income tax provision in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970601" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">fourth</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970602" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017.</em> The Company continues to maintain a full valuation allowance on the deferred tax assets in California, primarily due to a low apportionment factor and the amount of R&amp;D tax credits generated is greater than the amount utilized.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:0.9pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Other </b></i><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><i><b>Income Tax Provision </b></i><i><b>Matters</b></i></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, the Company did <em data-td-fact-id="25970603" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have federal net operating loss carryforwards. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the state net operating loss carryforwards for income tax purposes were <em data-td-fact-id="25970604" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$19.7</em> million, which will expire beginning in <em data-td-fact-id="25970605" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2028.</em> </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, the Company had R&amp;D tax credit carryforwards of <em data-td-fact-id="25970606" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.8</em> million for federal income tax purposes, which will begin to expire in <em data-td-fact-id="25970607" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2037,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970608" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$21.0</em> million for state income tax purposes, which can be carried forward indefinitely. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:0pt;margin-right:15pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">Upon adoption of ASU<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> <em data-td-fact-id="25970609" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="25970610" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="25970611" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em><i> </i><i>Compensation&mdash;Stock Compensation (Topic <em data-td-fact-id="25970612" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">718</em>): Improvements to Employee Share-Bas</i><i>ed Payment Accounting, </i>on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>all excess tax benefits and deficiencies related to equity awards are recognized in the income tax provision in the Consolidated Statements of Operations prospectively, rather than in additional paid-in-capital in the Consolidated Balance Sheets. In addition, the standard eliminates the requirement to defer recognition of excess tax benefits until they are realized through a reduction to income taxes payable. </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">The Company applied the modified retrospective method and there was <em data-td-fact-id="25970613" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no net</em> adjustment to retained earnings as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2017, </em>as the deferred tax assets related to the previously unrecognized excess tax benefits were fully offset by a valuation allowance. The Company expects increased volatility to the income tax provision in future periods dependent upon, among other variables, the price of its common stock and the timing and volume of equity award vesting. </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In the event of a change in ownership, as defined under federal and state tax laws, the Company&#39;s net operating loss and tax credit carryforwards could be subject to annual limitations.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;The annual limitations could result in the expiration of the net operating loss and tax credit carryforwards prior to utilization.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> July 27, 2015, </em>in<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;<i>Altera Corp. v. Commissioner</i>, the U.S. Tax Court issued an opinion related to the treatment of stock-based compensation expense in an intercompany cost-sharing arrangement. A final decision was issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 2015, </em>and the IRS appealed the decision in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016. </em>At this time, the U.S. Department of the Treasury has <em data-td-fact-id="25970614" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> withdrawn the requirement from its regulations to include stock-based compensation </font>in the cost pool to be shared under a cost-sharing arrangement<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">. Due to the uncertainty surrounding the status of the current regulations, questions related to the scope of potential benefits, and the risk of the Tax Court&rsquo;s decision being overturned upon appeal, the Company has <em data-td-fact-id="25970615" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> recorded any adjustments as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em>The Company will continue to monitor developments related to this opinion and the potential impact on its financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>the Company had $<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970616" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">16.3</em> million of unrecognized tax benefits, <em data-td-fact-id="25970617" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9.1</em> million of which would affect its effective tax rate if recognized after considering the valuation allowance. At <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2016, </em>the Company had <em data-td-fact-id="25970618" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$14.4</em> million of unrecognized tax benefits, <em data-td-fact-id="25970619" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.5</em> million of which would affect its effective tax rate if recognized after considering the valuation allowance.&nbsp; </font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">71</div>

			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; margin-left: 9pt;">Increase for tax position of current year</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="25969618" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2,360</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
		</tr>
		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; margin-left: 9pt;">Decrease due to lapse of statute of limitation</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 1px;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em data-td-fact-id="25969619" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(688</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; padding-bottom: 1px;">)</td>
		</tr>
		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(204, 238, 255);">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Balance as of December 31, 2017</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 16%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em data-td-fact-id="25969620" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">16,272</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company recognizes interest and penalties, if any, related to uncertain tax positions in its income tax provision. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, </em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><em data-td-fact-id="25970620" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970621" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the Company has <em data-td-fact-id="25970622" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.5</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970623" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.3</em> million, respectively, of accrued interest related to uncertain tax positions, which were recorded in long-term income tax liabilities in the Consolidated Balance Sheets.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">Uncertain tax positions relate to the allocation of income and deductions among the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s global entities and to the determination of the research and development tax credit. It is reasonably possible that over the next <em data-td-fact-id="25970624" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em>-month period, the Company <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>experience increases or decreases in its unrecognized tax benefits. However, it is <em data-td-fact-id="25970625" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> possible to determine either the magnitude or the range of increases or decreases at this time.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company currently has reduced tax rates in its subsidiaries in<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"> Chengdu and Hangzhou, China for performing research and development activities through <em data-td-fact-id="25970626" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2020</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970627" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019,</em> respectively. In addition, the Company currently has a tax holiday in Switzerland, which allows for tax-free operations through <em data-td-fact-id="25970628" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018.</em> &nbsp;The tax holiday and tax incentives had an insignificant impact on earnings per share for the periods presented.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Income Tax Examinations</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is subject to examination of its income tax returns by the IRS and other tax authorities. The Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s U.S. Federal income tax return for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2014 </em>was under examination by the IRS in <em data-td-fact-id="25970629" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 2017, </em>the IRS completed its examination with <em data-td-fact-id="25970630" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> adjustments.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s U.S. Federal income tax returns for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2005 </em>through <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2007 </em>were under examination by the IRS. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2011, </em>the Company received from the IRS a Notice of Proposed Adjustment (&quot;NOPA&quot;) relating to a cost-sharing agreement entered into by the Company and its international subsidiaries on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2004. </em>In the NOPA, the IRS objected to the Company&rsquo;s allocation of certain litigation expenses between the Company and its international subsidiaries and the amount of &quot;buy-in payments&quot; made by the international subsidiaries to the Company in connection with the cost-sharing agreement, and proposed to increase the Company&rsquo;s U.S. taxable income according to a few alternative methodologies. In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2012, </em>the Company received a revised NOPA from the IRS (&ldquo;Revised NOPA&rdquo;). In this Revised NOPA, the IRS raised the same issues as in the NOPA issued in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2011 </em>but under a different methodology. Under the Revised NOPA, the largest potential federal income tax payment, if the IRS were to prevail on all matters in dispute, was <em data-td-fact-id="25970631" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$10.5</em> million, plus interest and penalties, if any. The Company responded to the Revised NOPA in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> May 2012. </em>In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2013, </em>the IRS responded and continued to disagree with the Company&rsquo;s rebuttal. The Company met with the IRS Office of Appeals in <em data-td-fact-id="25970632" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> and both parties engaged in continuous discussions for a resolution of the matter in the <em data-td-fact-id="25970633" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970634" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015.</em> Meanwhile, the Company granted the IRS an extension of the statute of limitations for taxable years <em data-td-fact-id="25970635" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2005</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="25970636" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2007</em> to <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> September 30, 2015.&nbsp;</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The IRS also audited the research and development credits carried forward into year <em data-td-fact-id="25970637" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2005</em> and the credits generated in the years <em data-td-fact-id="25970638" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2005</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="25970639" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2007.</em> The Company received a NOPA from the IRS in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2011, </em>proposing to reduce the research and development credits generated in years <em data-td-fact-id="25970640" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2005</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="25970641" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2007</em> and the carryforwards, which would then reduce the value of such credits carried forward to subsequent tax years.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">In <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2015, </em>the Company reached a final resolution with the IRS in connection with the income tax audits for the years <em data-td-fact-id="25970642" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2005</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="25970643" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2007.</em> Under the agreement, the Company made a <em data-td-fact-id="25970644" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-time buy-in payment of <em data-td-fact-id="25970645" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.2</em> million for taxes related primarily to the revaluation of a license for certain intellectual property rights of the Company to <em data-td-fact-id="25970646" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> of its international subsidiaries.<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp; This buy-in payment was final and <em data-td-fact-id="25970647" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> additional payment would be required with respect to the intellectual property license for the years under examination or for a previous or subsequent tax year. In addition, the Company made an interest payment of <em data-td-fact-id="25970648" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.0</em> million as well as a tax payment of <em data-td-fact-id="25970649" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.1</em> million for the tax years <em data-td-fact-id="25970650" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2008</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="25970651" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2013</em> in <em data-td-fact-id="25970652" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015.</em>&nbsp; There were <em data-td-fact-id="25970653" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> penalties assessed on the Company as a result of the audits.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin-bottom:0pt;margin-left:9pt;margin-right:0pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">72</div>

</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: Times New Roman, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">For the <em data-td-fact-id="25970654" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">second</em> quarter of <em data-td-fact-id="25970655" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> the Company&#39;s income tax provision included a <em data-td-fact-id="25970656" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-t<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ime net charge of <em data-td-fact-id="25970657" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.7</em> million reflecting the taxes and interest, partially offset by the reversal of previously accrued tax liabilities and valuation allowances. Of the <em data-td-fact-id="25970658" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.7</em> million charge, <em data-td-fact-id="25970659" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.6</em> million was related to taxes and <em data-td-fact-id="25970660" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.1</em> million was related to interest.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>
</div>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Lease Obligations</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017</em><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">, future minimum payments under the non-cancelable operating leases were as follows (in thousands):</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company leases warehouse space, sales and marketing, and research and development offices in China, India, Japan, Korea, the United States and Europe. Certain of the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s facility leases provide for periodic rent increases. Rent expense for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970663" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970664" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> was <em data-td-fact-id="25970665" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.5</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="25970666" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.7</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="25970667" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.8</em> million, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><i><b>Warranty and Indemnification Provisions</b></i></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The changes in warranty reserves <font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">are as follows (in thousands):</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

</div>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company provides indemnification agreements to certain direct or indirect customers. The Company agrees to reimburse these parties for any damages, costs and expenses incurred by them as a result of legal actions taken against them by <em data-td-fact-id="25970668" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em> parties for infringing upon their intellectual property rights as a result of using the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s products and technologies. These indemnification provisions are varied in their scope and are subject to certain terms, conditions, limitations and exclusions. In addition, the Company has entered into indemnification agreements with its directors and officers.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">It is <em data-td-fact-id="25970669" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> possible to predict the maximum potential amount of future payments under these agreements due to the limited history of indemnification claims and the unique facts and circumstances involved in each particular agreement. There were <em data-td-fact-id="25970670" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> indemnification liabilities incurred in any of the periods presented. However, there can be <em data-td-fact-id="25970671" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> assurances that the Company will <em data-td-fact-id="25970672" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> incur any financial liabilities in the future as a result of these obligations.</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6349" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132519245" id="thunderdome-statement-770211">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970673" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970674" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em></b><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp;L</b><b>ITIGATION</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company is a party to actions and proceedings in the ordinary course of business, including potential litigation initiated by its shareholders, challenges to the enforceability or validity of its intellectual property, claims that the Company<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&rsquo;s products infringe on the intellectual property rights of others, and employment matters. These proceedings often involve complex questions of fact and law and <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>require the expenditure of significant funds and the diversion of other resources to prosecute and defend. The Company defends itself vigorously against any such claims. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em>there were <em data-td-fact-id="25970675" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> material pending legal proceedings to which the Company was a party.</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
<div id="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">73</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6350" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132527494" id="thunderdome-statement-770212">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970676" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970677" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em></b><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp;</b><b>EMPLOYEE <em data-td-fact-id="25970678" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">401</em>(k)</b><b> PLAN</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Compa<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">ny sponsors a <em data-td-fact-id="25970679" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">401</em>(k) retirement savings plan for all employees in the United States who meet certain eligibility requirements. Participants <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>contribute up to the amount allowable as a deduction for federal income tax purposes. The Company is <em data-td-fact-id="25970680" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> required to contribute and did <em data-td-fact-id="25970681" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> contribute to the plan for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017, </em><em data-td-fact-id="25970682" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="25970683" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015.</em></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="6351" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1519132537174" id="thunderdome-statement-770213">
<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970684" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em></b><font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;"><b><em data-td-fact-id="25970685" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6</em></b><b>.&nbsp;&nbsp;</b><b>SIGNIFICANT</b><b> CUSTOMERS</b></font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">The Company sells its products primarily through <em data-td-fact-id="25970686" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em>-party distributors and value-added resellers, and directly to original equipment manufacturers, original design manufacturers and electronic manufacturing service providers. The following table summarizes<font style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">&nbsp;those customers with sales equal to or greater than <em data-td-fact-id="25970687" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> of the Company&#39;s total revenue or with accounts receivable balances greater than <em data-td-fact-id="25970688" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">10%</em> of the Company&rsquo;s total accounts receivable:</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman, Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&nbsp;</p>

			<td id=".lead.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
